cancellationReason: User judged that self-sufficient data generation is unnecessary and that finding public data is preferable, based on the measured throughput estimates (180k positions would need ~2 days of exclusive execution; 25M would need ~9 months).

generator.determinismDesign: time_ms is always None (never wall-clock-based) so identical seed+config reproduces byte-identical output; a node-count safety net (max-nodes-midgame/max-nodes-exact) bounds worst-case per-move cost deterministically after an unbounded design was found to let a single game run over 13 minutes without completing during pilot preparation
generator.resumeDesign: checkpoint stores completed game count + a config fingerprint; on mismatch the tool refuses to continue rather than silently mixing data from different settings; output file is appended once per completed game

manualVerification.determinismAtProductionSettings: seed=1, games=3, full production config (depth=12 etc.) run twice independently; output files byte-identical (diff empty)
manualVerification.resumeAtProductionSettings: seed=42, games=8, killed after game 1 completed (SIGKILL), output file intact (60 lines, no truncated/corrupt line), restarted with same args, correctly skipped game 1 and continued from game 2 (checkpoint gamesCompleted advanced 1 -> 2, output file grew to 120 lines with no duplication)

run.note: Positions-per-game was exactly 60 for every observed game (Othello's maximum move count; strong play on both sides did not produce early full-wipeout endings in this sample).

qualitySignal.reason: Cancelled before reaching a meaningful scale; only 1,980 positions were generated (33 games) when the user decision arrived, far short of the 180k target that alone would need ~2 days.
